original_hypothesis.title: BlastOnboardingBoot bootstrap() Creates Pool with Unvalidated Fee Rate Model
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: BlastOnboardingBoot.bootstrap() calls router.createPool() which calls factory.create() using the factory's current maintainerFeeRateModel. If the factory owner changes the maintainerFeeRateModel after BlastOnboardingBoot.initialize() but before bootstrap(), the created pool could use a malicious fee rate model that extracts excessive maintainer fees from all swaps, draining LP value.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: bootstrap() relies on factory's state at execution time, not at initialization time. The factory owner and BlastOnboarding owner may be different entities, creating a trust gap. The fee rate model controls both LP and maintainer fee splits.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deployment script MIMSwapLaunch.s.sol shows factory is deployed separately from bootstrapper; factory owner can change maintainerFeeRateModel at any time

proof_of_signal.pattern: bootstrap() calls router.createPool which calls factory.create; factory.create uses current factory.maintainerFeeRateModel which can be changed by factory owner between initialize() and bootstrap()

preconditions: Factory owner changes maintainerFeeRateModel after bootstrapper initialized
Factory owner is different entity from BlastOnboarding owner
Malicious fee rate model extracts excessive fees

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Factory.create passes address(maintainerFeeRateModel) to IMagicLP.init; no validation in bootstrap
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Factory owner front-runs bootstrap with setMaintainerFeeRateModel(maliciousModel)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Malicious fee model extracts excessive mtFee from every swap, draining LP value over time

economic_questions: Is the factory owner the same as the BlastOnboarding owner?
What percentage of swap value can mtFee extract?
Can the fee model be changed after pool creation?
validation_requirements: Verify factory owner identity from deployment scripts
Test pool creation with malicious fee rate model
Check if pool's fee rate model is immutable after init

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Continuous fee extraction from all pool swaps
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Up to 100% of swap fees redirected to maintainer
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Permanent once pool created with malicious model

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: All LLE participants' funds subject to excessive fees

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All LLE participants who deposited MIM/USDB
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Ongoing fee extraction from LP value

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Factory ownership, maintainerFeeRateModel address, fee rate bounds

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
